[30;1m200 | [0m[35mother interpretation of the glob pattern.  Thus, a pattern like[0m
[30;1m201 | [0m[35m`+(a|{b),c)}`, which would not be valid in bash or zsh, is expanded[0m

[30;1m184 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m185 | [0m[35mThe double-star character `**` is supported by default, unless the[0m
[30;1m186 | [0m[35m`noglobstar` flag is set.  This is supported in the manner of bsdglob[0m
[30;1m187 | [0m[35mand bash 4.1, where `**` only has special significance if it is the only[0m
[30;1m188 | [0m[35mthing in a path part.  That is, `a/**/b` will match `a/x/y/b`, but[0m
[30;1m189 | [0m[35m`a/**b` will not.  **Note that this is different from the way that `**` is[0m
[30;1m190 | [0m[35mhandled by ruby's `Dir` class.**[0m

[30;1m337 | [0m[35m      <a href="http://github.com/documentcloud/underscore/">Underscore</a> is a[0m
[30;1m338 | [0m[35m      utility-belt library for JavaScript that provides a lot of the[0m
[30;1m339 | [0m[35m      functional programming support that you would expect in[0m

[30;1m24 | [0m[35mIf the global option `-g` is specified, the binary `shjs` will be installed. This makes it possible [0m
[30;1m25 | [0m[35mrun ShellJS scripts much like any shell script from the command line, i.e. without requiring a `node[0m
[30;1m26 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m27 | [0m[35m```bash[0m
[30;1m28 | [0m[35m$ shjs my_script[0m

[30;1m16 | [0m[35m  queue until new slots become available. If 1024 turns out to be too[0m
[30;1m17 | [0m[35m  much, it decreases the limit further.[0m
[30;1m18 | [0m[35m* fixes `lchmod` for Node versions prior to 0.6.2.[0m
[30;1m19 | [0m[35m* implements `fs.lutimes` if possible. Otherwise it becomes a noop.[0m
[30;1m20 | [0m[35m* ignores `EINVAL` and `EPERM` errors in `chown`, `fchown` or[0m
[30;1m21 | [0m[35m  `lchown` if the user isn't root.[0m

[30;1m136 | [0m[35m#### emitter.many(event, timesToListen, listener)[0m
[30;1m137 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m138 | [0m[35mAdds a listener that will execute **n times** for the event before being removed. The listener is in[0m

[30;1m8 | [0m[35mQUnit is especially useful for regression testing: Whenever a bug is reported,[0m
[30;1m9 | [0m[35mwrite a test that asserts the existence of that particular bug. Then fix it and[0m
[30;1m10 | [0m[35mcommit both. Every time you work on the code again, run the tests. If the bug[0m
[30;1m11 | [0m[35mcomes up again - a regression - you'll spot it immediately and know how to fix[0m
[30;1m12 | [0m[35mit, because you know what code you just changed.[0m

[30;1m107 | [0m[35m* `negate` True if the pattern is negated.[0m

[30;1m69 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m70 | [0m[35mIf brace expansion is not disabled, then it is performed before any[0m
[30;1m71 | [0m[35mother interpretation of the glob pattern.  Thus, a pattern like[0m
[30;1m72 | [0m[35m`+(a|{b),c)}`, which would not be valid in bash or zsh, is expanded[0m
[30;1m73 | [0m[35m**first** into the set of `+(a|b)` and `+(a|c)`, and those patterns are[0m
[30;1m74 | [0m[35mchecked for validity.  Since those two are valid, matching proceeds.[0m
[30;1m75 | [0m[35m[0m

[30;1m56 | [0m[35mThe double-star character `**` is supported by default, unless the[0m

[30;1m58 | [0m[35mand bash 4.1, where `**` only has special significance if it is the only[0m
[30;1m59 | [0m[35mthing in a path part.  That is, `a/**/b` will match `a/x/y/b`, but[0m
[30;1m60 | [0m[35m`a/**b` will not.  **Note that this is different from the way that `**` is[0m

[30;1m104 | [0m[35m* `regexp` Created by the `makeRe` method.  A single regular expression[0m
[30;1m105 | [0m[35m  expressing the entire pattern.  This is useful in cases where you wish[0m
[30;1m106 | [0m[35m  to use the pattern somewhat like `fnmatch(3)` with `FNM_PATH` enabled.[0m
[30;1m107 | [0m[35m* `negate` True if the pattern is negated.[0m
[30;1m108 | [0m[35m* `comment` True if the pattern is a comment.[0m
[30;1m109 | [0m[35m* `empty` True if the pattern is `""`.[0m

[30;1m70 | [0m[35mIf brace expansion is not disabled, then it is performed before any[0m
[30;1m71 | [0m[35mother interpretation of the glob pattern.  Thus, a pattern like[0m
[30;1m72 | [0m[35m`+(a|{b),c)}`, which would not be valid in bash or zsh, is expanded[0m
[30;1m73 | [0m[35m**first** into the set of `+(a|b)` and `+(a|c)`, and those patterns are[0m
[30;1m74 | [0m[35mchecked for validity.  Since those two are valid, matching proceeds.[0m
[30;1m75 | [0m[35m[0m

[30;1m200 | [0m[35m## Slicing[0m
[30;1m201 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m202 | [0m[35mNode programs are called with more or less the exact argv as it appears[0m
[30;1m203 | [0m[35min C land, after the v8 and node-specific options have been plucked off.[0m

[30;1m205 | [0m[35mJavaScript program being run.[0m
